children.value: is a lightweight, high-performance mobile table engine specifically designed for the Vue 3 ecosystem.
It solves two major developer pain points:
the scarcity of mature mobile-native table components
the subpar user experience when using desktop-oriented components on mobile devices

Through its minimalist directive-based integration, a single line of code is all it takes to empower any PC table component—such as Element Plus or Ant Design Vue—with a native-grade, silky-smooth mobile interaction experience.

— Utilizes Delta Time frame-rate normalization to eliminate speed variances across different refresh rates, ensuring a consistent, silky-smooth feel on any Hz screen. Built-in intelligent inertia protection identifies finger pauses to prevent accidental inertial triggers.

— Millisecond-level intent determination filters diagonal jitter. Operations are locked to a single axis (horizontal or vertical) throughout the gesture, eliminating the "diagonal drift" pain point when browsing long tables.

— Automatically detects scroll boundaries. When reaching the edge, control is smoothly handed back to the browser, maintaining full compatibility with system-level native interactions like iOS slide-to-back.

— Smartly distinguishes between "braking" and "triggering." Click events are intercepted only during high-speed inertia to provide immediate stabilization, preventing accidental clicks while flicking through data.

— Locks onto active fingers using Touch ID to eliminate coordinate jumps during finger handover. When multi-touch gestures (e.g., pinch-to-zoom) are detected, the engine enters a silent mode, allowing the browser to handle native behaviors.
— Built-in presets for over ten major UI libraries, including Element Plus, Ant Design Vue, Naive UI, and VxeTable. Achieve deep interaction optimization with just a single line of code.

— An optimized state machine for hybrid devices like Surface and touch-screen laptops. Offers zero-intrusion for pure mouse operations, activating on-demand only for touch inputs to ensure seamless transitions between input methods.
scenarios. Automatically corrects touch trajectories via intelligent axis mapping, ensuring touch directions align perfectly with visual intuition without additional computational overhead.
— Implemented in pure TypeScript with no third-party package dependencies, ensuring a clean and stable core logic.

The Vue ecosystem still lacks mobile Table solutions that can stand alongside mainstream desktop table stacks. For complex flows such as reporting, mobile also needs high-performance tables with full interaction capabilities, but that gap remains obvious today.

In practice teams usually take one of two paths: build a table in-house, where touch interaction, scrolling mechanics, and advanced features are extremely expensive to implement and maintain; or reuse desktop-oriented stacks such as Element Plus tables, which are not optimized for mobile and often feel wrong on touch and small screens.
When reusing desktop table components on mobile, common pain points include:
— horizontal data scanning unintentionally triggers vertical page scrolling due to imprecise finger paths
— slight finger tilt causes frequent axis switching and a shaky scroll path
often lags in deeply nested UI structures
— scrolling stops too abruptly or feels stiff after release, requiring repeated swipes
— fixed headers fail to keep pixel-level sync with body scrolling
— refresh-rate differences produce inconsistent inertia feel across devices
— multi-finger coordinate jumps or interference with native gestures such as iOS swipe-back
— aggressive hijacking on Surface/touch laptops can hurt mouse and trackpad users
landscape layouts misalign physical touch direction and logical scroll direction
addresses these by intercepting touch flow and running an in-directive scroll physics engine; see

The directive intercepts touch event flow and runs a complete in-directive scroll physics pipeline, including axis decision, velocity sampling, inertia decay, edge yielding, and hybrid-device strategies for stable mobile behavior.
for the full mechanism breakdown and implementation details.

Complex mobile data views such as wide tables, financial reports, and reconciliation sheets. You need horizontal dragging, vertical browsing, sorting, and nested tables within a small viewport, which demands strong table capabilities, touch feel, and rendering performance.
The same business screens must work on both desktop and mobile, delivering predictable interaction without rewriting table business logic.
for mechanism details and design rationale
This page covers common usage patterns for the
directive. For complete configuration options and type definitions, see

props.code: npm install vue3-mobile-table
yarn add vue3-mobile-table
pnpm add vue3-mobile-table

import { createApp } from 'vue'
import App from './App.vue'
import Vue3MobileTable from 'vue3-mobile-table'

const app = createApp(App)
app.use(Vue3MobileTable)
app.mount('#app')

property, which has the same effect:
to the directive are equivalent — both fully destroy the directive instance (removing all event listeners and restoring original styles). You do
need to use this for device detection; the directive's
which automatically detects the device type and applies the optimal strategy:
: Dormant — no event binding, zero overhead
: Lazy hijacking — only activates after confirming a real touch drag, avoiding interference with mouse/trackpad users

By default, the directive detects when scrolling reaches the container boundary and hands control back to the browser, enabling native gestures like iOS swipe-back. In fullscreen overlay scenarios (e.g. landscape mode), this is undesirable. Set

Only enable this when the table is inside a fullscreen overlay or a context where browser-native edge gestures should be suppressed. In normal page layouts, edge detection improves UX by allowing swipe-back and outer scroll.
to simulate landscape mode on mobile, the touch coordinate axes no longer align with the element's logical axes. The
parameter tells the directive to apply an inverse coordinate transform:
. For a complete landscape toggle example, see the

// Scroll ended: trigger analytics, load more data, etc.

contents.title: Options & Types
contents.description: Option tables, TypeScript types, and library exports for Vue3 Mobile Table.

children.value: A Vue 3 custom directive that adds touch scroll support to table elements. In templates, use the
attribute. For global registration and local import, see the
Configuration options interface for the directive.
Whether to enable the directive. When
, all event listeners and style hijacking are fully cleaned up (equivalent to passing
UI library preset name, automatically applies the corresponding scroll container selector
CSS selector for the target scroll element, takes priority over
Friction/decay rate. Higher = slower decay (smoother scrolling), lower = faster stop (range: 0.8 - 0.99)
Displacement threshold (px) before determining scroll direction, distinguishes minor jitter from intentional dragging
Whether to disable inertia scrolling. When
, scrolling stops immediately on finger release
Speed threshold (px/ms) for emergency stop click blocking. Higher = more lenient, lower = more strict (range: 0.3 - 1.0)
: automatic detection with lazy hijacking for hybrid devices.
: always activate touch handling regardless of device type
CSS rotation angle (degrees). When the container uses CSS
to simulate landscape mode, the directive applies an inverse transform to touch coordinates for correct scroll mapping
Whether to disable edge detection. When
, the directive will not hand control back to the browser when scrolling reaches the boundary. Useful for fullscreen overlays where native gestures (e.g. iOS swipe-back) are unwanted
Callback fired when scrolling starts, after finger displacement exceeds
Callback fired when scrolling ends, after inertia animation fully stops

props.code:
interface MobileTableOptions {
  enabled?: boolean
  preset?: TablePreset
  selector?: string
  friction?: number
  dragThreshold?: number
  disableInertia?: boolean
  clickBlockThreshold?: number
  mode?: 'auto' | 'always'
  rotation?: 0 | 90 | -90 | 180
  disableEdgeDetection?: boolean
  onScrollStart?: () => void
  onScrollEnd?: () => void
}

if the preset does not exist.

Velocity threshold (px/ms) to stop inertia animation
Max frame interval limit (ms), prevents jumps after frame drops

Default speed threshold (px/ms) for emergency stop click blocking

contents.title: Other Libraries
contents.description: Using Vue3 Mobile Table with other UI libraries or custom tables

children.value: Vue3 Mobile Table supports Table components from all desktop Vue UI component libraries.
For libraries with built-in presets, see
For libraries without built-in presets, use
to specify the actual scrollable container element inside the table. You'll need to use browser DevTools to find the CSS selector for the DOM element responsible for scrolling:
If you'd like to add built-in preset support for a UI library, feel free to submit an Issue or PR on

Many PC-oriented table components have a large number of columns. When viewed on a mobile device in portrait orientation, the horizontal space is insufficient, and users must scroll extensively to see all the data. Rotating to landscape orientation effectively swaps the screen's width and height, providing a much wider viewport for table content.
There are several ways to achieve a landscape layout on mobile, but each has significant trade-offs:
Requires the page to be in fullscreen mode first (
). Most browsers reject the lock outside of fullscreen or user-initiated events, and iOS Safari does not support it at all
Force users to rotate the device
Relies on the user's physical action and the device's auto-rotate setting. Cannot be triggered programmatically, and many users keep rotation lock enabled
Requires completely redesigning the table layout for narrow screens — high cost, and may not suit data-dense tables that inherently need many columns
avoids all of the above problems:
— no API restrictions, no permission prompts
— triggered by a button tap, no reliance on device orientation
— no need to redesign columns or break the PC table structure
— GPU-composited transform, no reflow or repaint
The only downside is that after CSS rotation, the physical touch axes and the element's logical scroll axes become misaligned. That is exactly what the
solves — it automatically applies an inverse coordinate transform to correct the touch mapping.
) so the rotated container fills the screen
, aligning scroll direction with the rotated table's logical axes
This example needs to be experienced on a mobile device. Click the button to enter the full-screen landscape mode.
is the key positioning technique. After rotating 90° clockwise around the top-left corner, the container shifts off-screen to the left. The
pushes it back, making it cover the entire viewport precisely.
Disable Edge Detection in Landscape Mode
In landscape mode, it's recommended to set

. The main reason is that while horizontally browsing wide tables, gestures frequently reach the screen edges. If edge detection remains enabled, gestures may be released at the boundary and trigger native system events (such as back navigation gestures). Disabling edge detection keeps control within the table scroll logic and helps prevent accidental system-level actions.
If you toggle landscape by state, you can also write it explicitly:
On devices with notches or rounded corners (e.g., iPhone), apply safe-area padding directly to the landscape container:

When your project uses a framework with built-in responsive font scaling (e.g., font size automatically increases on larger screens), rotating to landscape mode triggers this scaling. However, if table column widths are fixed in pixels, they won't scale proportionally, causing column headers to wrap or content to overflow.
Portrait mode: font-size = 14px, column width = 100px (looks balanced)
Landscape mode: font-size = 18px (auto-scaled), column width = 100px (unchanged) → header text wraps
Use a helper function that calculates column width based on both viewport width and a maximum constraint:
// Remove this comment line when using in your project
* Calculate responsive width that scales with viewport but respects a max constraint
- Base width in pixels (at ideal width)
function to your table column widths:
This approach ensures that as the viewport (and font size) grows, column widths scale proportionally, maintaining visual balance across all screen sizes.

links.text: Why CSS transform: rotate() Instead of Alternatives?

children.text: Disable Edge Detection in Landscape Mode

children.value: intercepts touch event flow and implements a complete in-directive scroll physics engine, covering gesture direction decision, velocity sampling, inertia decay, edge yielding, and device-specific strategies so desktop table components stay stable and follow-finger on mobile.
Automatically determines gesture intent when displacement exceeds the threshold, then locks the gesture to a single axis for its entire duration:

Uses sliding window sampling instead of traditional weighted averages, keeping only touch points from the last 50ms to calculate release velocity. This fundamentally eliminates sampling jitter on low-end devices and over-dense sampling on high refresh rate screens:
Physics decay during inertia is normalized via Delta Time, ensuring the same swipe produces identical scroll distances regardless of screen refresh rate:
Combined with dirty value detection, proactively dispatches
events to force immediate header synchronization, solving the visual tearing caused by asynchronous native
Automatically yields control when scrolling reaches container boundaries, including detection for both horizontal and vertical axes and scenarios where no scroll is needed:

props.code:
if (isHorizontal) {
  const maxScroll = el.scrollWidth - el.clientWidth
  const atLeft = el.scrollLeft <= 1 && dx > 0
  const atRight = el.scrollLeft >= maxScroll - 1 && dx < 0
  const noScroll = el.scrollWidth <= el.clientWidth
  if (atLeft || atRight || noScroll) isAtEdge = true
}

children.value: Uses a four-state machine to handle mixed environments — pure PC stays dormant, mobile activates immediately, and hybrid devices use sentinel events that only trigger full hijacking after confirming a real touch drag:
// Standby: only sentinel events, no style changes
// pending-active: touchstart recorded, but styles not hijacked yet
// active: overflow: hidden applied, full touch event binding

vue3-mobile-table is a lightweight interaction extension engine specifically designed to adapt Vue 3 desktop table components for mobile devices. Perfectly compatible with mainstream UI libraries like Element Plus, Ant Design Vue, and VxeTable, it empowers PC tables with a native-grade, silky-smooth interaction experience on mobile devices with just a single line of code.
是一款专为 Vue 3 生态设计的轻量化、高性能的移动端表格引擎。
成熟的移动端表格组件匮乏
成熟的桌面端组件在移动端使用时体验糟糕
通过极简的指令式接入，只需一行代码，即可赋予 Element Plus、Ant Design Vue 等任意 PC 端表格组件原生级的丝滑移动端交互体验。
— 采用基于 Delta Time 的帧率归一化技术，彻底消除不同屏幕刷新率带来的速度差异，在任意 HZ 屏幕下均能提供一致的丝滑体感。内置智能惯性保护：识别手指停顿意图，有效防止误触发惯性滑动。
手势意图与方向轴锁定
— 毫秒级判定手势意图，自动过滤对角线抖动。在整个手势期间将操作锁定在单一轴向（横向或纵向），彻底解决长表格浏览过程中的「斜向漂移」痛点。
— 自动探测滚动边界。当滚动触及尽头时，平滑地将事件控制权交还给浏览器，完美兼容 iOS 侧滑返回等系统级原生交互。
— 智能区分「刹车」与「触发」。仅在高速惯性滚动中拦截点击事件以实现即时避震，防止在翻阅数据时发生误触。
多指追踪与手势自适应
— 基于 Touch ID 锁定活跃指代，消除多指交替时的坐标跳变。检测到多指手势（如缩放）时，引擎自动进入静默模式，由浏览器接管原生行为。
主流 UI 库深度适配
— 内置 Element Plus、Ant Design Vue、Naive UI、VxeTable 等十余款主流组件库的预设配置，一行代码即可实现深度的交互优化。
— 针对 Surface/触屏笔记本等混合设备优化的状态机。纯鼠标操作零侵入，仅在触摸触发时按需唤醒，确保不同输入方式间的无缝切换。
场景。通过坐标轴智能映射自动修正触摸轨迹，确保横屏模式下的滑动方向与操作直觉完全一致，且无额外计算开销。
— 纯原生 TypeScript 实现，无任何第三方包依赖。
Vue 生态中尚未出现可对标主流桌面表格栈的移动端 Table 方案。在报表等复杂业务场景下，移动端同样需要具备高性能与完整交互能力的表格组件，但当前生态存在明显空缺。
实际落地通常只有两种路径：其一，自研表格组件，但触控交互、滚动机制与复杂功能实现成本极高，开发与维护负担沉重；其二，复用 Element Plus 等桌面端表格组件，但由于未针对移动端优化，在触控与小屏场景下普遍存在体验问题。
复用桌面表格时，常见痛点包括：
— 用户本想横向查看表格数据，却因手指路径不直而意外触发页面纵向滚动，导致视线丢失
— 手指轻微倾斜就可能在横纵轴之间频繁切换，滑动路径显得杂乱
— 在复杂 UI 组件嵌套场景中，原生
— 松手后滚动立即中断或惯性生硬，远距离浏览需要反复拖拽
— 表体滚动时固定表头无法像素级同步，出现错位或延迟
— 刷新率差异导致同一套代码在不同设备上手感不一致
— 多指操作坐标跳变，或边界处干扰 iOS 侧滑返回等系统手势
— 在 Surface、触屏笔记本等设备上盲目劫持会影响鼠标与触控板使用
CSS 旋转横屏坐标错乱
场景下触摸物理方向与逻辑滚动方向错位
通过接管触摸事件流，在指令内以滚动物理引擎应对上述问题；实现细节见
该指令通过接管触摸事件流，在内部实现方向判定、速度采样、惯性衰减、边缘交还与混合设备策略等完整滚动物理机制，保证移动端滚动既跟手又稳定。
中查看完整机制说明与关键实现思路。
面向移动端的宽表、财务报表、对账清单等复杂数据展示场景。需要在有限屏幕内支持横向拖拽、纵向浏览、排序及嵌套表等多维交互，对表格功能、触控体验与渲染性能要求较高。
同一业务页面需同时覆盖 PC 与移动端，在不重写表格业务逻辑的前提下，实现一致且可预期的交互体验。
从安装到高级选项完成配置
了解滚动机制与设计思路
指令的常见用法。完整的配置项和类型定义请参考

props.code: npm install vue3-mobile-table
yarn add vue3-mobile-table
pnpm add vue3-mobile-table

import { createApp } from 'vue'
import App from './App.vue'
import Vue3MobileTable from 'vue3-mobile-table'

const app = createApp(App)
app.use(Vue3MobileTable)
app.mount('#app')

UI 库预设名称，自动应用对应 UI 库的滚动容器选择器
目标滚动元素的 CSS 选择器，优先级高于
摩擦力/衰减率，值越大衰减越慢（滚动越丝滑），值越小即停感越强（建议范围：0.8 - 0.99）
判定滚动方向前的位移阈值 (px)，用于区分轻微抖动点击和有意识的拖拽
急停点击拦截的速度阈值 (px/ms)，值越大越宽松，值越小越严格（建议范围：0.3 - 1.0）
：自动检测，混合设备启用懒接管。
：始终激活触摸处理，忽略设备类型
CSS 旋转角度（度数）。当容器通过 CSS
模拟横屏时，指令会对触摸坐标进行逆变换以正确映射滚动方向
时，滚动到达边界后不会交还控制权给浏览器，始终由指令接管。适用于全屏覆盖层等不希望触发浏览器原生手势（如 iOS 侧滑返回）的场景

props.code:
interface MobileTableOptions {
  enabled?: boolean
  preset?: TablePreset
  selector?: string
  friction?: number
  dragThreshold?: number
  disableInertia?: boolean
  clickBlockThreshold?: number
  mode?: 'auto' | 'always'
  rotation?: 0 | 90 | -90 | 180
  disableEdgeDetection?: boolean
  onScrollStart?: () => void
  onScrollEnd?: () => void
}

contents.title: 其他库
contents.description: Vue3 Mobile Table 支持所有桌面端 Vue3 UI 组件库中的 Table 组件。

children.value: 已内置预设的组件库请参阅
对于没有内置预设的组件库，可以通过
指定表格内部实际可滚动的容器元素。你需要通过浏览器开发者工具找到该表格组件中负责滚动的 DOM 元素的 CSS 选择器：
如果你希望为某个组件库添加内置预设支持，欢迎在
上提交 Issue 或 PR。

html .default .shiki span {color: var(--shiki-default);background: var(--shiki-default-bg);font-style: var(--shiki-default-font-style);font-weight: var(--shiki-default-font-weight);text-decoration: var(--shiki-default-text-decoration);}html .shiki span {color: var(--shiki-default);background: var(--shiki-default-bg);font-style: var(--shiki-default-font-style);font-weight: var(--shiki-default-font-weight);text-decoration: var(--shiki-default-text-decoration);}html .dark .shiki span {color: var(--shiki-dark);background: var(--shiki-dark-bg);font-style: var(--shiki-dark-font-style);font-weight: var(--shiki-dark-font-weight);text-decoration: var(--shiki-dark-text-decoration);}html.dark .shiki span {color: var(--shiki-dark);background: var(--shiki-dark-bg);font-style: var(--shiki-dark-font-style);font-weight: var(--shiki-dark-font-weight);text-decoration: var(--shiki-dark-text-decoration);}
许多 PC 端表格组件拥有大量列字段。在移动端竖屏模式下，横向空间严重不足，用户需要频繁横向滑动才能查看完整数据。旋转到横屏方向可以有效地交换屏幕的宽高，为表格内容提供更宽的视口。
实现移动端横屏有多种方式，但每种都有明显的局限性：
需要页面先进入全屏模式（
）。大多数浏览器在非全屏或非用户手势触发时会拒绝锁定，且 iOS Safari 完全不支持此 API
依赖用户的物理操作和设备的自动旋转设置，无法通过代码触发，且许多用户会锁定屏幕方向
需要为窄屏完全重新设计表格布局——成本高昂，且对于天然需要多列的数据密集型表格并不适用
— 无 API 限制，无权限弹窗
— 通过按钮点击触发，不依赖设备方向
— 无需重新设计列结构，PC 端表格原样复用
— GPU 合成层变换，不触发重排或重绘
唯一的副作用是 CSS 旋转后，触摸的物理轴向与元素的逻辑滚动轴向不再对齐。这正是
参数所解决的——它会自动对触摸坐标进行逆变换矫正，使滑动方向与旋转后的表格逻辑方向一致。
，从而使滚动方向与旋转后的表格逻辑轴对齐
此示例需要在移动端设备中体验。点击按钮即可进入全屏横屏模式。
是关键的定位技巧。绕左上角顺时针旋转 90° 后，容器会偏移到屏幕左侧之外，而
将其推回，使其刚好覆盖整个视口。

props.id: 横屏时建议关闭边缘检测

children.value: 横屏时建议关闭边缘检测
。主要原因是：在横向浏览宽表时，手势经常会滑到屏幕边缘；如果继续保留边缘检测，手势可能在边缘被释放并触发系统原生事件（例如返回手势）。关闭边缘检测后，边缘滑动会继续由表格滚动逻辑接管，避免误触发系统级操作。
如果你是通过状态切换横屏，也可以显式写成：
在带有刘海屏或圆角的设备（如 iPhone）上，建议将安全区应用到横屏容器本身：
当你的项目使用框架自带的响应式字体缩放（例如字体大小会根据屏幕尺寸自动增大）时，旋转到横屏模式会触发这种缩放。但如果表格列宽是固定的像素值，它们不会按比例增大，导致列标题换行或内容溢出。
竖屏模式：font-size = 14px，列宽 = 100px（视觉平衡）
横屏模式：font-size = 18px（自动缩放），列宽 = 100px（未变化）→ 标题文本换行
使用一个辅助函数，根据视口宽度和最大宽度约束来动态计算列宽：
// 使用中请删掉下面这行注释
// 理想宽度，设计稿的宽度（移动端参考宽度）
// 表示伸缩视图的最大宽度
* 限制大小的 vw 转换
- 基础宽度（像素，基于理想宽度）
这种方法确保随着视口（和字体大小）增大，列宽按比例缩放，在所有屏幕尺寸上保持视觉平衡。

children.id: 横屏时建议关闭边缘检测
children.text: 横屏时建议关闭边缘检测

children.value: 通过接管触摸事件流，在指令内部实现方向判定、速度采样、惯性衰减、边缘交还与设备策略，构建一套完整的滚动物理引擎，让桌面表格在移动端依然保持稳定、跟手、可预期的交互体验。
当手指位移超过阈值时自动判定手势意图，整个手势期间锁定在单一轴向：
采用滑动窗口采样替代传统的加权平均，仅保留最近 50ms 内的触摸点来计算松手速度，从根源上消除低端机采样抖动和高刷屏幕采样过密的影响：
惯性阶段的物理衰减基于 Delta Time 归一化，使得同一次滑动在任意刷新率的屏幕上都产生相同的滚动距离：
配合脏值检测，主动派发
事件强制表头即时同步，解决原生
当滚动触达容器边界时自动交还控制权，同时覆盖横向和纵向检测以及无需滚动的场景：

props.code:
if (isHorizontal) {
  const maxScroll = el.scrollWidth - el.clientWidth
  const atLeft = el.scrollLeft <= 1 && dx > 0
  const atRight = el.scrollLeft >= maxScroll - 1 && dx < 0
  const noScroll = el.scrollWidth <= el.clientWidth
  if (atLeft || atRight || noScroll) isAtEdge = true
}

children.value: 通过四态状态机处理混合环境 — 纯 PC 保持休眠，移动端立即激活，混合设备使用哨兵事件，仅在确认真实触摸拖拽后才触发完整劫持：
// standby：仅哨兵事件，不修改样式
// pending-active：touchstart 已记录，但样式未劫持
// active：overflow: hidden 已应用，绑定全套触摸事件

html .default .shiki span {color: var(--shiki-default);background: var(--shiki-default-bg);font-style: var(--shiki-default-font-style);font-weight: var(--shiki-default-font-weight);text-decoration: var(--shiki-default-text-decoration);}html .shiki span {color: var(--shiki-default);background: var(--shiki-default-bg);font-style: var(--shiki-default-font-style);font-weight: var(--shiki-default-font-weight);text-decoration: var(--shiki-default-text-decoration);}html .dark .shiki span {color: var(--shiki-dark);background: var(--shiki-dark-bg);font-style: var(--shiki-dark-font-style);font-weight: var(--shiki-dark-font-weight);text-decoration: var(--shiki-dark-text-decoration);}html.dark .shiki span {color: var(--shiki-dark);background: var(--shiki-dark-bg);font-style: var(--shiki-dark-font-style);font-weight: var(--shiki-dark-font-weight);text-decoration: var(--shiki-dark-text-decoration);}
vue3-mobile-table 是一款专为 Vue3 桌面端表格组件适配移动端设备所打造的轻量级交互扩展引擎。完美兼容 Element Plus、Ant Design Vue 与 VxeTable 等主流组件库，只需一行代码，即可让 PC 端表格在移动端设备上具有原生级的丝滑交互体验。
